statement.p: A rectangles base is {1\ {\rm in}} shorter than twice its height. The rectangles area is {6\ {\rm in^{2}}}. Find this rectangles dimensions.

solution.p: Let the rectangles height be x inches. Then its base is 2x-1 inches.
Its given that the rectangles area is {6\ {\rm in^{2}}}, so we can write and solve the equation:
The polynomial can be factored, so we will first solve this equation by factoring:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] (2x+3)(x-2) \amp =0 \\ 2x+3=0 \amp \text{ or } x-2=0 \\ 2x=-3 \amp \text{ or } x=2 \\ x=\text{a negative number} \amp \text{ or } x=2 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We could also solve the equation by the Quadratic Formula.
Notice that there is no need to calculate the negative result, as a rectangles height cannot be negative. The height is {2\ {\rm in}}.
Recall that the rectangles base is 2x-1 inches, where x is the height. Thus the base is 2 \cdot {2\ {\rm in}} - {1\ {\rm in}}, which is {3\ {\rm in}}.
So the rectangles height is {2\ {\rm in}}, and its base is {3\ {\rm in}}.
